title: Sarga 106
summary: Rama's divine birth, childhood, protection of Sage Vishwamitra's rituals from demons, breaking of Shiva's bow, and marriage to Sita. Addresses youth, childhood, education, overcoming early obstacles, and marriage.

sections.summary: This passage begins with “Seeing Rama with his face downcast and distressed, like the moon submerged in sorrow, Lakshmana, with a firm resolve, spoke gentle words to him.” and develops toward “Having heard the words full of righteousness and purpose from those assembled in the midst of the council, Rama spoke to Lakshmana.”

This passage begins with “O son of Sumitra, I shall abandon you, lest there be a transgression of dharma, for abandonment or killing is prescribed, and for the virtuous, both are considered equal.” and develops toward “Then, having seen the fourth part of Vishnu (Lakshmana) arrive, all the best of gods, along with the great sages, were greatly delighted and…”

verses.sanskrit: अवाङ्मुखमथो दीनं दृष्ट्वा सोममिवाप्लुतम् । राघवं लक्ष्मणो वाक्यं हृष्टो मधुरमब्रवीत् ।। 7.106.1 ।।
verses.translation: Seeing Rama with his face downcast and distressed, like the moon submerged in sorrow, Lakshmana, with a firm resolve, spoke gentle words to him.

verses.sanskrit: न संतापं महाबाहो मदर्थं कर्तुमर्हसि । पूर्वनिर्माणबद्धा हि कालस्य गतिरीदृशी ।। 7.106.2 ।।
verses.translation: O mighty-armed one, you should not grieve for my sake, for such is indeed the course of time, bound by preordained destiny.

verses.sanskrit: जहि मां सौम्य विस्रब्धं प्रतिज्ञां परिपालय । हीनप्रतिज्ञाः काकुत्स्थ प्रयान्ति नरकं नराः ।। 7.106.3 ।।
verses.translation: O gentle one, confidently abandon me and fulfill your vow, for men who fail in their promises, O Kakutstha, go to hell.

verses.sanskrit: यदि प्रीतिर्महाराज यद्यनुग्राह्यता मयि । जहि मां निर्विशङ्कस्त्वं धर्मं वर्धय राघव ।। 7.106.4 ।।
verses.translation: O great king, if there is affection and favor towards me, then abandon me without hesitation, O Raghava, and uphold your dharma.

verses.sanskrit: लक्ष्मणेन तथोक्तस्तु रामः प्रचलितेन्द्रियः । मन्त्रिणः समुपानीय तथैव च पुरोधसम् ।। 7.106.5 ।।
verses.translation: Thus spoken to by Lakshmana, Rama, with his senses greatly disturbed, summoned his ministers and similarly his chief priest.

verses.sanskrit: अब्रवीच्च तदा वृत्तं तेषां मध्ये स राघवः । दुर्वासोभिगमं चैव प्रतिज्ञां तापसस्य च ।। 7.106.6 ।।
verses.translation: Then, in their midst, Rama recounted the incident, including the arrival of Durvasa and the ascetic's vow.

verses.sanskrit: तच्छ्रुत्वा मन्त्रिणः सर्वे सोपाध्यायाः समासत । वसिष्ठस्तु महातेजा वाक्यमेतदुवाच ह ।। 7.106.7 ।।
verses.translation: Having heard that, all the ministers, along with their preceptors, assembled, and then the greatly effulgent Vasishtha spoke these words.

verses.sanskrit: दृष्टमेतन्महाबाहो क्षयं ते रोमहर्षणम् । लक्ष्मणेन वियोगश्च तव राम महायशः ।। 7.106.8 ।।
verses.translation: O mighty-armed and greatly renowned Rama, this terrifying destruction and your separation from Lakshmana have been foreseen.

verses.sanskrit: त्यजैनं बलवान्कालो मा प्रतिज्ञां वृथा कृताः । विनष्टायां प्रतिज्ञायां धर्मो ऽपि च लयं व्रजेत् ।। 7.106.9 ।।
verses.translation: Abandon him, for time is powerful; do not let your vow be made in vain, because if the vow is violated, even righteousness will meet its dissolution.

verses.sanskrit: ततो धर्मे विनष्टे तु त्रैलोक्यं सचराचरम् । सदेवर्षिगणं सर्वं विनश्येत्तु न संशयः ।। 7.106.10 ।।
verses.translation: Therefore, if righteousness is destroyed, then without a doubt, all three worlds, including all moving and non-moving beings, along with the groups of gods and sages, would also be destroyed.

verses.sanskrit: स त्वं पुरुषशार्दूल त्रैलोक्यस्याभिपालनात् । लक्ष्मणेन विना चाद्य जगत्स्वस्थं कुरुष्व ह ।। 7.106.11 ।।
verses.translation: O tiger among men, for the protection of the three worlds, you must now make the world stable even without Lakshmana.

verses.sanskrit: तेषां तत्समवेतानां वाक्यं धर्मार्थसंहितम् । श्रुत्वा परिषदो मध्ये रामो लक्ष्मणमब्रवीत् ।। 7.106.12 ।।
verses.translation: Having heard the words full of righteousness and purpose from those assembled in the midst of the council, Rama spoke to Lakshmana.

verses.sanskrit: विसर्जये त्वां सौमित्रे मा भूद्धर्मविपर्ययः । त्यागो वधो वा विहितः साधूनां तूभयं समम् ।। 7.106.13 ।।
verses.translation: O son of Sumitra, I shall abandon you, lest there be a transgression of dharma, for abandonment or killing is prescribed, and for the virtuous, both are considered equal.

verses.sanskrit: रामेण भाषिते वाक्ये बाष्पव्याकुलितेन्द्रियः । लक्ष्मणस्त्वरितं प्रायात्स्वगृहं न विवेश ह ।। 7.106.14 ।।
verses.translation: When these words were spoken by Rama, Lakshmana, whose senses were agitated by tears, quickly departed and indeed did not enter his own house.

verses.sanskrit: स गत्वा सरयूतीरमुपस्पृश्य कृताञ्जलिः । निगृह्य सर्वस्रोतांसि निःश्वासं न मुमोच ह ।। 7.106.15 ।।
verses.translation: Having gone to the bank of the Sarayu, he performed ritual purification, folded his hands in reverence, and restraining all his vital breaths, he indeed did not release a single exhalation.

verses.sanskrit: अनिःश्वसन्तं युक्तं तं सशक्राः साप्सरोगणाः । देवाः सर्षिगणाः सर्वे पुष्पैरभ्यकिरंस्तदा ।। 7.106.16 ।।
verses.translation: Then, all the gods, along with Indra, groups of Apsaras, and groups of sages, showered flowers upon him (Lakshmana), who was absorbed (in his divine form) and had ceased breathing.

verses.sanskrit: अदृश्यं सर्वमनुजैः सशरीरं महाबलम् । प्रगृह्य लक्ष्मणं शक्रस्त्रिदिवं संविवेश ह ।। 7.106.17 ।।
verses.translation: Indeed, Indra took the mighty Lakshmana, who was invisible to all humans and still in his physical body, and entered heaven.

verses.sanskrit: ततो विष्णोश्चतुर्भागमागतं सुरसत्तमाः । दृष्ट्वा प्रमुदिताः सर्वे ऽपूजयन् समहर्षयः ।। 7.106.18 ।।
verses.translation: Then, having seen the fourth part of Vishnu (Lakshmana) arrive, all the best of gods, along with the great sages, were greatly delighted and worshipped him. इत्यार्षे श्रीमद्रामायणे वाल्मीकीये आदिकाव्ये श्रीमदुत्तरकाण्डे षडुत्तरशततमः सर्गः ।। 106 ।। Thus ends the hundred and sixth sarga of Uttara Kanda of the holy Ramayana the first epic composed by sage Valmiki.
